title: Chinese open-source models Kimi K3 and Qwen 3.8 challenge Western AI leaders
summary: China launches advanced language models that surprise with their performance, competing with systems like Claude and ChatGPT, while other sectors prepare for the impact of AI.

title: Jensen Huang's signed leather jacket auctioned for almost one million dollars
summary: The iconic jacket of Nvidia's CEO reaches $960,000 in a charity auction, far above its initial valuation.

title: AMD Ryzen 7 7700X3D drops to $279 just days after launch
summary: The mid-range X3D processor receives a quick discount, making it a very attractive option for the mid-range segment.

title: Eleven fans and liquid cooling for an RTX 3080: the mod that lowers 33 °C but barely adds FPS
summary: An enthusiast subjects his graphics card to extreme modification, managing to drastically reduce temperature with a minimal performance increase.

title: From the 2004 DARPA race to Waymo's autonomous taxis: the evolution of self-driving vehicles
summary: Autonomous cars have come a long way from the early competitions in the desert to the current services resuming activity in San Francisco.

title: Protests erupt across the United States against the expansion of artificial intelligence data centers
summary: More than 120 demonstrations in 37 states denounce the use of eminent domain and the environmental impact of new data centers.

title: Flock vehicle surveillance cameras spark controversy over police abuses and citizen attacks
summary: License plate recognition devices are vandalized, while officers lose their jobs for using them to harass, and they are expanded into urban surveillance networks.

title: Facebook and Instagram suffer a massive global outage leaving accounts inaccessible
summary: A technical failure prevents access to Meta's platforms for several hours, affecting millions of users.

title: Google DeepMind argues that video generators already contain the world models that computer vision sought
summary: The company defends that video models can predict physics and interactions, surpassing traditional vision systems.

title: Meta warns that current human-oriented infrastructure may have only 20 months before being rebuilt for AI agents
summary: The company points out the urgency of adapting digital infrastructure to the rise of autonomous artificial intelligence agents.

title: India fines HP $14.4 million for rigging public tenders and fixing prices
summary: The investigation found that HP rigged government tenders and agreed on ink and toner prices with other companies.

title: WhatsApp blocks sex workers' accounts in the Netherlands, leaving them without income
summary: The messaging platform's decision affects sex workers who relied on the app to contact clients.

title: Lawsuit accuses ChatGPT of inducing a woman in Alabama to suicide with false divine prophecies
summary: The victim's family claims that OpenAI's chatbot convinced her to end her life to fulfill an alleged religious purpose.

title: France and Germany announce intention to develop European rival to Palantir analytics software
summary: Both countries seek to strengthen their technological sovereignty with a local alternative to the U.S. data intelligence platform.

title: Netflix acquires Ben Affleck's artificial intelligence startup for $587 million
summary: The streaming platform buys InterPositive, an AI company founded by the actor, to boost content creation.

title: Artificial intelligence algorithm assigns harsher living conditions to Black inmates in Ontario prisons
summary: The classification system used in Canadian prisons systematically discriminates against inmates of African descent, according to an investigation.

title: Spotify removes 75 million AI-generated songs and doesn't stop there
summary: The music streaming platform continues its fight against synthetic content to protect copyright and artistic quality.

title: The AI frenzy cools and tech stocks suffer sharp declines
summary: The stock market reflects growing skepticism toward AI promises, causing steep drops in valuations of sector companies.

title: Peter Thiel details his controversial 'Antichrist' theory and criticizes tech and climate regulators
summary: The billionaire investor outlines his ideas on how overregulation is stifling innovation and human progress.

title: FCC accepted gifts worth over $63,000 from Paramount while evaluating its deals
summary: The regulatory commission chairman received tickets and other gifts from CBS/Paramount at critical times for merger approval.
